content: Dry-run mode: the write node is a no-op. Classification runs normally, but proposals go to `state/staging/dry-run/` instead of being applied to Octowiki. You can inspect every proposal and see what the tree would have done. When you trust the classifier, flip a flag and proposals go to `state/staging/pending/` for auto-commit. Build order: deterministic evaluator first (no LLM, just filesystem checks producing next-action.json), then the agent prompt around it.

provenance.raw_agent: Dry-run just means the write node is a no-op. The classification runs, the proposal is written to staging/dry-run/ instead of being applied. What to build first: The deterministic evaluator — it has no LLM, it's just file system checks producing a next-action.json.
provenance.raw_user: I think we should build some of the ground work for running the behaviour tree as a dry run.
